content:
First, place the big circle \(B\) of radius \(2008\) so that its center is at \((0,2008)\) and it is tangent to the \(x\)–axis (line \(A\)) at the origin \(P=(0,0)\).  A smaller circle of radius \(r\) tangent to the \(x\)–axis has its center at \((x,r)\) with 
\[
x^{2}+(r-2008)^{2}=(r+2008)^{2}
\;\Longrightarrow\;
x=2\sqrt{2008\,r}.
\]
If two such circles of radii \(r_{n-1}=a\) and \(r_n=b\) sit in a chain, tangent to each other and to the big circle, one finds after some algebra the recurrence
\[
r_n
=\;a\;\frac{\bigl(2008+\sqrt{2008\,a}\bigr)^2}{(2008-a)^2}\,.
\]
It is much cleaner, however, to set 
\[
x_n=\sqrt{r_n},\qquad  
X_n=\frac{x_n}{\sqrt{2008}}.
\]
Then one shows
\[
X_{n+1}=\frac{X_n}{1-X_n},
\]
with
\[
X_1=\frac{\sqrt{1}}{\sqrt{2008}}=\frac1{\sqrt{2008}}\,, 
\]
so
\[
\frac1{X_{n+1}}
=\frac{1-X_n}{X_n}
=\frac1{X_n}-1,
\]
whence
\[
\frac1{X_n}=\frac1{X_1}-(n-1)
=\sqrt{2008}-(n-1).
\]
Thus
\[
X_n=\frac1{\sqrt{2008}-(n-1)},
\qquad
r_n=2008\,X_n^2=\frac{2008}{\bigl(\sqrt{2008}-(n-1)\bigr)^2}.
\]
For the \(n\)th circle still to fit under the big circle, we need \(r_n<2008\), i.e.
\[
\frac{2008}{(\sqrt{2008}-(n-1))^2}<2008
\;\Longleftrightarrow\;
\bigl|\sqrt{2008}-(n-1)\bigr|>1.
\]
Since \(\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82\), the strongest restriction is
\[
\sqrt{2008}-(n-1)>1
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
n-1<\sqrt{2008}-1\approx43.82
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
n\le44.
\]
At \(n=45\) the formula gives \(r_{45}>2008\), which is impossible.  Hence the largest \(n\) is

\[
\boxed{44}.
\]

x=2\sqrt{2008\,r}.

r_n
=\;a\;\frac{\bigl(2008+\sqrt{2008\,a}\bigr)^2}{(2008-a)^2}\,.
